problem_statement.framework: I am / Trying to / But / Because / Feels
problem_statement.i_am: an adult seeking mental health support
problem_statement.trying_to: find a therapist who truly understands my needs and with whom I'll build a lasting therapeutic relationship
problem_statement.but: the current system offers either overwhelming directories with no matching intelligence or subscription services that randomly assign therapists
problem_statement.because: there is no data-driven way to assess therapeutic compatibility before committing time, money, and emotional energy
problem_statement.feels: anxious about making the wrong choice, frustrated by the trial-and-error process, and increasingly likely to abandon therapy altogether
problem_statement.impact: 50% of therapy clients quit after just 3 sessions due to poor therapeutic fit. This represents a $38.7B annual waste in the US mental health system.
problem_statement.root_cause: The mental health marketplace lacks a compatibility signal -- there is no 'Zestimate for therapy' that helps clients and therapists evaluate fit before the first session.

personas.name: Sarah
personas.goal: Find a therapist for anxiety who 'gets' her
personas.pain: Tried 3 therapists, none were good fits, $1,200 wasted
personas.tech: Daily smartphone user, comfortable with apps

jtbd.primary_job: Help me find a therapist I'll actually connect with
jtbd.social: Show I'm taking meaningful action on my mental health
jtbd.emotional: Feel confident I'm making the right choice, reduce anxiety about starting therapy

journey.awareness: Realizes therapy need, searches 'find therapist near me', overwhelmed by options
journey.research: Compares directories, reads profiles, can't assess compatibility, frustration peaks
journey.selection: Picks based on photo/bio, hopes for the best, high anxiety about fit
journey.engagement: Attends sessions, evaluates fit, 50% chance of mismatch, considers switching
journey.advocacy: If matched well, long-term relationship, refers friends, NPS 60+

personas.name: Dr. Marcus
personas.goal: Fill practice with well-matched clients to reduce burnout
personas.pain: 40% of new clients drop after intake, wasting session slots
personas.tech: Uses EHR, open to new platforms that don't add admin burden

jtbd.primary_job: Help me fill my practice with well-matched clients
jtbd.emotional: Reduce burnout from mismatched clients, feel professional fulfillment

personas.name: Jennifer
personas.goal: Provide employees with quality mental health benefits, track ROI
personas.pain: Current EAP has 3% utilization, can't prove wellness investment value

jtbd.primary_job: Help me prove our mental health investment works
jtbd.social: Demonstrate leadership commitment to employee wellness
jtbd.emotional: Confidence that benefits budget is well-spent

personas.name: Alex
personas.goal: Maintain platform health, manage compliance, scale infrastructure
personas.pain: Manual therapist verification, no automated quality monitoring

positioning.who: struggle to find the right therapist
positioning.that: uses assessment-driven matching to connect clients with their ideal therapist
positioning.unlike: BetterHelp and Psychology Today which offer either random assignment or overwhelming directories
positioning.differentiator: Lily AI provides a Therapeutic Zestimate -- a data-driven compatibility score that predicts therapeutic fit before the first session
positioning.core_identity: The Zillow of Therapy -- bringing data transparency to the mental health marketplace
positioning.positioning_axis: Matching Intelligence (low to high) vs. User Agency (low to high) -- Lily AI occupies the high-high quadrant
positioning.full_statement: For adults navigating mental health care who struggle to find the right therapist, Lily AI is the therapeutic compatibility platform that uses assessment-driven matching to connect clients with their ideal therapist. Unlike BetterHelp and Psychology Today which offer either random assignment or overwhelming directories, Lily AI provides a Therapeutic Zestimate -- a data-driven compatibility score that predicts therapeutic fit before the first session.

top_opportunity.description: Minimize the time and emotional cost of finding the right therapist
top_opportunity.current_state: Over-served by directories, under-served by matching intelligence

tam.basis: US adults with mental health conditions (57.8M) x average annual therapy spend ($1,340)

sam.basis: Adults who would use a digital matching platform (18.2M) x blended platform ARPU ($490/yr)

market_size.growth_trajectory: SOM expands to $340M by Year 5 with geographic expansion and insurance integration

pestel.political: Mental Health Parity Act enforcement expanding; state telehealth licensing reciprocity improving (PSYPACT covers 40+ states); HIPAA enforcement increasing
pestel.economic: $280B global mental health market growing 15% CAGR; employer mental health spending up 38% since 2020; therapy session costs rising 5-7% annually
pestel.social: Post-COVID therapy normalization (destigmatization); Gen Z/Millennial preference for digital-first healthcare; growing demand for culturally competent therapists
pestel.technological: AI matching algorithms maturing; telehealth infrastructure commoditized; assessment psychometrics validated for digital delivery
pestel.environmental: Remote work enabling location-independent therapy access; climate anxiety emerging as therapy driver
pestel.legal: AI in healthcare regulation emerging (EU AI Act, state-level); data privacy requirements increasing (CCPA, state health data laws); professional licensing portability improving

competitive_landscape.problem_hypothesis: Therapist-client matching is broken because there is no compatibility signal in the mental health marketplace.
validation_evidence: 50% therapy dropout rate in first 3 sessions (APA research)
No competitor offers assessment-driven compatibility scoring
competitive_landscape.go_no_go: GO -- validated unmet need with no direct competitor addressing compatibility matching. The 'Therapeutic Zestimate' concept has no equivalent in market.
competitive_landscape.lily_positioning_quadrant: High Matching Intelligence + High User Agency (unique position)

key_insights: Therapists use Facebook groups as a manual matching engine -- posting multi-dimensional referral requests (specialty + insurance + demographics + modality + geography) that get buried in feeds. Lily's Quiz > Match funnel automates exactly this workflow.
853 referral request posts (14.5% of 5,869) from 17 FB groups represent the core use case Lily replaces.
6 of 8 top implicit feature requests from FB groups are ALREADY BUILT in Lily (75% pre-launch feature coverage).
Top pain point: 239 posts about pricing transparency; 183 posts about inability to find specialists.
50% of therapy clients quit after 3 sessions due to poor therapeutic fit -- $38.7B annual waste.
No competitor offers assessment-driven compatibility scoring -- the 'Therapeutic Zestimate' has no market equivalent.
Top opportunity (ODI Score 14.2): Minimize the time and emotional cost of finding the right therapist.
FB group geographic concentration in PA (318), CA (96), FL (88), NY (76) suggests strong East Coast launch opportunity.
411 posts (7%) signal demand for availability checking; 386 posts (6.6%) signal demand for demographic matching -- both already built.
Therapist-to-client referral is the highest-scoring acquisition channel (9.0/10) with $0 CAC and $568 LTV.

validated_hypotheses.name: Therapeutic Zestimate
validated_hypotheses.hypothesis: IF we implement compatibility scoring using assessment data, THEN match satisfaction increases 40%+ (vs 25% with basic matching).

validated_hypotheses.name: Progressive Assessment Funnel
validated_hypotheses.hypothesis: IF we convert quiz library into progressive onboarding, THEN completion rate reaches 60%+ and profiles are rich enough for matching.

validated_hypotheses.name: Supply-First Metro Launch
validated_hypotheses.hypothesis: IF we achieve 50+ verified therapists per top-10 metro, THEN we can support consumer acquisition without empty-shelf UX.
validated_hypotheses.kill_criteria: CAC per therapist > $100 or sign-up rate < 5%.

validated_hypotheses.name: Org ROI Dashboard
validated_hypotheses.hypothesis: IF we show measurable therapy utilization and wellness outcomes, THEN org contract renewal rate exceeds 85%.

validated_hypotheses.name: Coach AI Enhancement
validated_hypotheses.hypothesis: IF we add session memory and personalized coaching protocols, THEN between-session engagement increases 3x and therapy outcomes improve 15%.
validated_hypotheses.experiment: Coach AI v2 beta with 100 active users.

fb_research_summary.feature_coverage: 75% -- 6 of 8 top implicit feature requests already built
fb_research_summary.product_market_fit_signal: 853 referral request posts validate the core use case Lily replaces

look_inward: 72+ validated assessments that generate rich user profiles
Therapist profile data including modalities, specialties, approaches
Ability to compute compatibility scores from existing quiz data
look_outward: Clients need a trust signal before committing to a therapist
Therapists need pre-qualified referrals to reduce intake churn
Organizations need measurable outcomes to justify mental health spend
look_around: Zillow's Zestimate transformed real estate by providing a trust signal
Dating apps proved swipe-based matching works for human connection
No one has applied either pattern to therapeutic matching

opportunity_solution_tree.target_outcome: Increase therapy retention from 50% to 75% at 3 months
opportunity_solution_tree.sprint_focus: Opportunities 1-2 with Gate A validation at Week 12
